flashcards.definition: A positively charged ion (lost electrons); metals typically form cations (Na⁺, Ca²⁺).
A negatively charged ion (gained electrons); nonmetals typically form anions (Cl⁻, O²⁻).
A technique for writing formulas: swap the charges of the ions as subscripts.
A charged group of covalently bonded atoms (e.g., SO₄²⁻, NH₄⁺, NO₃⁻).
A representation showing the types and numbers of atoms in a compound (H₂O, NaCl).
